title: Lilium gongshanense
intro: Lilium gongshanense, also known as the Gongshan lily, is a species of lily native to the Himalayas and southwestern China, characterized by its pure white flowers and glossy green leaves. The Gongshan lily is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against certain diseases. Bioactive constituents of Lilium gongshanense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Gongshan lily is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Lilium gongshanense is a species of lily that belongs to the genus Lilium and the family Liliaceae. It is a type of bulbous perennial plant. The species is classified within the section Stageria of the genus Lilium. The classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its phylogenetic relationships with other lily species. Lilium gongshanense is native to China and is an important part of the country's biodiversity.
